mechanism_narrative: An extremely high unemployment rate signals a severe, ongoing economic contraction. This reduces expected corporate earnings and consumer demand, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and leading to negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE value of 14.7% is historically extreme, indicating a major economic shock.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE was above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at the effective lower bound provides maximum monetary accommodation. This lowers the discount rate for future earnings and encourages capital to flow into risk assets like equities, supporting positive market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% signals a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) environment.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When growth in retail sales (demand) significantly underperforms growth in industrial production (supply), it signals an unintended inventory build-up. This leads to future production cuts, earnings disappointments, and negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 3-month change in INDPRO by 2 percentage points
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in over 50% of triggered instances, indicating the market is not pricing in inventory risk.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production is a direct signal of strengthening economic activity in the goods-producing sector. This translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls is a primary indicator of a V-shaped recovery. This rapid re-employment boosts investor confidence and drives strong positive equity returns as worst-case recession scenarios are priced out.
evidence_refs: fact:High UNRATE suggests a post-shock environment where payroll changes are a key recovery signal.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 8%, the correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day S&P 500 forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accelerating inflation and decelerating real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment. This dynamic forces the central bank to choose between fighting inflation or supporting growth, creating policy uncertainty and compressing equity multiples.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown, as it affects construction, banking, and consumer durable goods. This contraction in a key cyclical sector leads to negative forward returns for the broader market.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls 20% below its 12-month average
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, suggesting the housing signal is not leading the equity market.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in the latest monthly retail sales data causes investors to upwardly revise near-term consumer demand and earnings forecasts, leading to positive equity market returns over a very short horizon as this new information is priced in.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a positive RSAFS trigger is not statistically different from the average return on all other days (t-test p > 0.10).
